# terrible_check_point_mgmt_cp_mgmt_access_rule (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) Object name.

### Optional

- `action` (String) a "Accept", "Drop", "Ask", "Inform", "Reject", "User Auth", "Client Auth", "Apply Layer".
- `action_settings` (String) Action settings.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `comments` (String) Comments string.
- `content` (String) List of processed file types that this rule applies on. Available from R80.10 management version.
- `content_direction` (String) On which direction the file types processing is applied. Available from R80.10 management version.
- `content_negate` (Boolean) True if negate is set for data. Available from R80.10 management version.
- `custom_fields` (String) Custom fields.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `destination` (String) Collection of Network objects identified by the name or UID.
- `destination_negate` (Boolean) True if negate is set for destination.
- `details_level` (String) The level of detail for some of the fields in the response can vary from showing only the UID value of the object to a fully detailed representation of the object.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Enable/Disable the rule.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `ignore_warnings` (Boolean) Apply changes ignoring warnings.
- `inline_layer` (String) Inline Layer identified by the name or UID. Relevant only if "Action" was set to "Apply Layer".
- `install_on` (String) Which Gateways identified by the name or UID to install the policy on.
- `layer` (String) Layer that the rule belongs to identified by the name or UID.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `position` (String) Position in the rulebase. The use of values "top" and "bottom" may not be idempotent.
- `relative_position` (String) Position in the rulebase. Use of this field may not be idempotent.
- `search_entire_rulebase` (Boolean) Whether to search the entire rulebase for a rule that's been edited in its relative_position field to make sure there indeed has been a change in its position or the section it might be in.
- `service` (String) Collection of Network objects identified by the name or UID.
- `service_negate` (Boolean) True if negate is set for service.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `source` (String) Collection of Network objects identified by the name or UID.
- `source_negate` (Boolean) True if negate is set for source.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `time` (String) List of time objects. For example, "Weekend", "Off-Work", "Every-Day".
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `track` (String) Track Settings. Available from R80.10 management version.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `user_check` (String) User check settings.
- `vpn` (String) Any or All_GwToGw.
- `vpn_list` (String) Communities or Directional.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `cp_mgmt_access_rule` (String) The checkpoint object created or updated.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
